title: fix: add OM runtime backpressure near the token threshold

similarOrDuplicatePRs.title: fix: refresh buffered chunk tokens before OM activation

summary: Adds runtime backpressure to observational memory (OM) when message tokens approach the configured threshold. Key changes: (1) new backpressure.ts module with tiered wait logic (0-4s based on ratio to threshold), message sealed-for-buffering detection, and projected-removal readiness checks; (2) pre-sealing of eligible unobserved messages shortly before threshold to give buffered activation cleaner swap points; (3) more aggressive async buffering intervals once pending tokens reach >=0.8x threshold (warm band) instead of the previous rampPoint approach; (4) updated and new tests covering warm-band intervals, pre-seal behavior, and threshold-crossing with backpressure. Changes are scoped to 4 files in packages/memory observational-memory module plus a changeset.
maintainerNotes: This is a draft PR from a maintainer (TylerBarnes) that is still in progress. CI has 2 failures (Agent Builder Tests, Observability Package Tests) which appear unrelated to the memory changes — this PR only modifies OM files. Memory Tests and E2E Tests are still pending. The PR is well-structured with a clear description, changeset, and tests. It builds on prior OM buffering fixes (#13476, #13955). The backpressure approach uses tiered waits with a short-circuit when buffered chunks already look activation-ready, which should avoid unnecessary blocking. Review should focus on the wait durations in resolveObservationBackpressureWaitMs (hardcoded 1000-4000ms tiers) and whether the warm-band threshold of 0.8x is appropriate. Wait for the author to mark it ready for review.
